title: Market Claim with Strong Copy but Weak Proof
rawSituation: We are launching a new product and the marketing team has prepared claims about market leadership and customer adoption. The actual customer data shows only 12 beta users, none of whom have completed the onboarding. The CEO wants to launch with the strong claims anyway. The product is not generating revenue yet.

kernelResult.situationSummary: This is an operational or release decision.
kernelResult.kernelInterpretation: The system interprets this situation as an operational or execution decision where delivery, capacity, or process reliability is the primary concern. Dimensions surfaced: evidence.

constraintGraph.description: Board approval required — decision cannot proceed without it

evidenceState.summary: Governance pressure — board-level decision with fiduciary implications

evidenceState.label: Constraint: Board approval required — decision cannot proceed without it
evidenceState.summary: Board approval required — decision cannot proceed without it

evidenceState.label: Evidence Warning: Unsupported claims
evidenceState.summary: Situation contains claims or allegations that may not be independently verifiable

evidenceState.summary: Claim is being made but evidence to support it is absent or weak

evidenceState.label: Condition: Rollback plan required
evidenceState.summary: No rollback or fallback plan is referenced. For any customer-facing launch, a tested rollback plan must exist before proceeding.

evidenceState.label: Condition: Launch approval required
evidenceState.summary: No launch approval or sign-off is referenced. Launching without authorisation creates accountability exposure.

adversarialChallenges.contradiction: Claim is being made but evidence to support it is absent or weak
adversarialChallenges.outputEffect: Output confidence downgraded. Human review recommended.

minimumViablePath.description: Address the primary identified risk: Claim is being made but evidence to support it is absent or weak. This is the failure point most likely to undermine the decision.
minimumViablePath.rationale: The adversarial challenge (claim-vs-evidence) must be resolved or consciously accepted before the decision proceeds.

minimumViablePath.description: Identify and confirm who holds formal decision mandate.
minimumViablePath.rationale: Without confirmed authority, the decision is vulnerable to challenge.

minimumViablePath.description: Verify: Board approval required — decision cannot proceed without it
minimumViablePath.rationale: Critical constraints change what is feasible.

forbiddenActions.action: Publish or present claims about market position, growth, or customer adoption without documented supporting evidence
forbiddenActions.reason: Claims made without supporting evidence create legal, regulatory, and reputational exposure. A claim that cannot be substantiated is a liability.

whatMustNotBeDelayed: Address the primary identified risk: Claim is being made but evidence to support it is absent or weak. This is the failure point most likely to undermine the decision.
